paper_map.title: Strong yet ductile low-density steel via multiphase heterogeneous microstructure
paper_map.abstract_summary: A low-density Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si steel is designed with multiphase heterogeneous microstructure to achieve high strength and ductility without costly microalloying. The study investigates the effects of intercritical annealing temperature and low bainite aging on the development of ferrite, bainite/tempered martensite, fresh martensite, and retained austenite. Mechanical testing reveals that the multi-level microstructure promotes heterogeneous deformation-induced strengthening and effectively mitigates stress concentration, resulting in a superior strength-ductility combination compared to conventional high-strength low-alloy steels. The work provides a pathway for lightweight structural applications via accessible thermo-mechanical processing.
paper_map.research_objective: To develop a low-density steel with a multiphase heterogeneous microstructure that simultaneously achieves high strength, good ductility, and effective stress concentration mitigation for lightweight structural applications through industrially feasible processing.
main_process_variables: vacuum induction melting and hot forging
hot rolling: reheating at 1200 °C/60 min, 6 passes from ~1100 °C to 880 °C, 90% reduction, air cooling
cold rolling: 60% reduction to 1.6 mm thickness
intercritical annealing at 750, 800, 850 °C for 30 min, air cooling
low bainite aging at 400 °C for 30 min
phase transformation temperatures: Ac1 = 705 °C, Ac3 = 950 °C, Bf = 380 °C, Ms = 346 °C
sample_aliases: AN750: cold-rolled + annealed at 750 °C for 30 min, air cooled
AN800: cold-rolled + annealed at 800 °C for 30 min, air cooled
AN850: cold-rolled + annealed at 850 °C for 30 min, air cooled
AN750-T: AN750 + low bainite aging at 400 °C for 30 min
AN800-T: AN800 + low bainite aging at 400 °C for 30 min
AN850-T: AN850 + low bainite aging at 400 °C for 30 min
key_sections: Introduction: motivation for lightweighting, limitations of Q&P and medium-Mn steels, proposal of multiphase heterogeneous design
Experimental: specimen preparation (melting, hot/cold rolling, annealing, aging), microstructure characterization techniques (SEM, EBSD, TEM, EPMA, XRD), mechanical tests
Results: microstructural features and mechanical properties of AN and AN-T series
Discussion: multiphase microstructure mechanical response including constitutive modeling and strengthening mechanism quantification, analysis of stress concentration mitigation
Conclusions: summary of structure-property relationships and industrial potential
